author: Zachary Proser
title: The RAG pipeline I'd actually let near production. With evals.
description: Production RAG end-to-end: chunking, embeddings, hybrid retrieval, reranking, and an eval loop you can defend in a code review. Updated for 2026.

commerce.paywallHeader: RAG that survives a real eval.
commerce.paywallBody: I led the first production RAG reference architecture at Pinecone, then spent two more years fixing the parts that broke under real traffic. This is the pipeline I'd build today: chunking with metadata, hybrid retrieval, a reranker, and an eval harness you can show your team. Notebook + Next.js app + tutorial + direct email support. Updated for 2026.
commerce.miniPaywallDescription: Notebook, Next.js app, eval harness, and a tutorial that won't insult your time.
commerce.paywallImageAlt: The complete RAG package: tutorial, Jupyter notebook, and Next.js example app

landing.heroTitle: The RAG pipeline I'd actually let near production.
landing.subtitle: Chunking, hybrid retrieval, a reranker, and an eval harness — Next.js + Pinecone, refined at Pinecone and still shipping.
landing.problemSolved: You got handed an LLM and a deadline. The team needs grounded answers, citations, and a way to prove it actually works.
landing.benefitStatement: End-to-end production RAG with the parts most tutorials skip: a reranker step, an eval loop with faithfulness and recoverability scores, and the failure modes I learned the hard way at Pinecone.

features.title: See your data pipeline, not a black box
features.description: The notebook walks every transformation: chunking strategies and the trade-offs, embedding choice (text-embedding-3-large vs voyage-3), and what Pinecone is actually doing under the hood. When retrieval gets weird in production, you'll know which knob to turn.

features.title: Hybrid retrieval + a reranker by default
features.description: Dense vector search is the floor, not the ceiling. The pipeline ships with hybrid retrieval (dense + BM25) and a Cohere reranker pass — the single biggest upgrade for grounding quality, and the part most tutorials never get to.

features.title: An eval harness you can defend in code review
features.description: Faithfulness, recoverability, answer relevance — measured per change, on your data. Ragas + LLM-as-judge wired into the same repo. The failure mode of a missed regression is now a failing CI step, not a Slack thread three weeks later.

features.title: Next.js app that streams, cites, and handles failure
features.description: Vercel AI SDK with streaming responses, inline citations, and the error paths you actually need: timeouts, empty retrievals, partial context, rate limits. The same patterns from the reference architecture I built at Pinecone, updated for the 2026 stack.

whatsIncluded.title: Jupyter notebook (chunking → embeddings → indexing)
whatsIncluded.description: The data-side of RAG that most tutorials hand-wave through. Loading and cleaning real sources, chunking strategies with the trade-offs spelled out, embedding model selection, and Pinecone indexing — annotated, runnable, and small enough to actually finish.
whatsIncluded.imageAlt: Jupyter notebook showing chunking, embeddings, and vector database indexing

whatsIncluded.title: Next.js app (streaming, citations, error paths)
whatsIncluded.description: Full source for a Vercel AI SDK app: streaming responses, inline citations, related content, and the error paths you'll actually hit — timeouts, empty retrievals, partial context, rate limits. One-click Vercel deploy or run it locally.
whatsIncluded.imageAlt: Next.js chat application with streaming responses and citations

whatsIncluded.title: Eval harness (faithfulness, recoverability, relevance)
whatsIncluded.description: Ragas + LLM-as-judge wired into the same repo, with a tiny CI script you can drop into any project. Run it on every prompt or pipeline change. Now you can prove your changes didn't quietly regress.
whatsIncluded.imageAlt: Eval harness output showing faithfulness, recoverability, and answer relevance metrics

whatsIncluded.title: Step-by-step tutorial (the why, not just the how)
whatsIncluded.description: The written guide that explains every decision: which embedding model and why, when chunking by semantic boundary beats fixed-size, when a reranker pays for itself, what to measure first. Customize it to your stack with confidence.

whatsIncluded.title: Direct email support
whatsIncluded.description: Ask me your questions as you build. I'll review issues, point you to the right pattern, and help you get to a pipeline you can put behind a real users.

experiencedEngineersSection.title: For senior engineers who got handed an LLM and a deadline.
experiencedEngineersSection.content:
Every RAG tutorial fails the same way:
- Toy examples with five documents that don't survive contact with real data
- Transformer theory you didn't ask for
- 40-hour courses when you have a sprint

You're already an engineer. You need the patterns, the working code, and a way to know when the pipeline is regressing.

This is what I'd build today after fifteen years shipping at Cloudflare, Gruntwork, Pinecone, and now WorkOS. Updated for 2026 — current models, hybrid retrieval, a reranker, and an eval loop.

landing.credibilityBanner: Built by Zack Proser · First production RAG reference architecture at Pinecone · Applied AI at WorkOS · Author of 'Vector Databases in Production for Busy Engineers'
